message:
feat(circleci): rewrite manifest to fcp-connector-manifest schema 2.1 and refine implementation

Overhaul the CircleCI connector manifest from the legacy format to the
current schema 2.1 standard with proper interface hashing, network
capabilities (dns, egress, tls.sni), zone configuration, and fully
enumerated operation definitions with risk levels and safety tiers.

README updated to reflect the authoritative first-slice operation surface
and clarify that CircleCI Server host overrides are a follow-on extension
(not part of the initial V3 contract).

Client and connector implementation refined to align with the manifest's
declared operation surface, error taxonomy updated for consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ The current connector code already exposes these operations:
 - `circleci.jobs.get`
 - `circleci.health`
 
-The current manifest does not fully describe that runtime surface yet. It only declares a subset of the operations, so bead `flywheel_connectors-j05nu.4.2.2` must reconcile manifest and runtime before the connector can claim a truthful V3 implementation.
+The current implementation bead is reconciling manifest and runtime so this operation inventory is now the authoritative first-slice surface.
 
 ## First-Slice Scope
 
@@ -51,16 +51,17 @@ The connector is `operational` and effectively stateless aside from configuratio
 
 - CircleCI API v2 supports personal API tokens, not project tokens.
 - The connector authenticates with the `Circle-Token` header and inherits the full read/write authority of that user token.
-- The public config contract is one `api_token`, plus `base_url`, retry policy, and bounded request timeout.
-- `base_url` defaults to `https://circleci.com/api/v2`. For CircleCI Server, the docs explicitly allow replacing `https://circleci.com` with the server hostname.
+- The public config contract for the first slice is one `api_token`, plus retry policy and bounded request timeout.
+- `base_url` exists as an implementation/test override, but the first-slice manifest and readiness contract only treat hosted `https://circleci.com/api/v2` as live-ready. CircleCI Server host overrides remain a follow-on extension.
 - The connector instance is user-scoped, not project-scoped. It can operate on any project the token can access unless a future allowlist is added.
 - Project-targeted operations require an explicit `project_slug`.
 - CircleCI documents `project_slug` as `vcs-slug/org-name/repo-name`; for GitLab and GitHub App projects the `vcs-slug` becomes `circleci`, with org and project IDs instead of human-readable names.
 - The current implementation implicitly tolerates an empty token as a secretless proxy-injection mode, but that is an implementation detail, not a stable public auth contract yet.
 
 ## Network And Runtime Invariants
 
-- Base API host: `circleci.com` by default, or a configured CircleCI Server host.
+- Base API host for the first production slice: `circleci.com`
+- The current manifest formalizes hosted `circleci.com` only; localhost overrides are test-only and CircleCI Server hosts are not yet encoded as a first-class V3 network contract.
 - Base path: `/api/v2`
 - Port: `443`
 - TLS + SNI required

@@ -1,135 +1,231 @@
-[format]
-type = "fcp-connector-manifest"
+[manifest]
+format = "fcp-connector-manifest"
 schema_version = "2.1"
+min_mesh_version = "2.0.0"
+min_protocol = "fcp2-sym/2.0"
+protocol_features = []
+max_datagram_bytes = 65000
+interface_hash = "blake3-256:fcp.interface.v2:9b4b96202c46d719724c3dd8ae1e8d4e1238aba2ee7e9917e131435b6c5a518c"
 
 [connector]
 id = "fcp.circleci"
 name = "CircleCI"
 version = "0.1.0"
-description = "CircleCI CI/CD platform connector for managing pipelines, workflows, and jobs"
-archetype = "operational"
-homepage = "https://circleci.com/docs/api/v2/"
+description = "CircleCI CI/CD connector for project discovery, pipeline and workflow control, job inspection, and health operations"
+archetypes = ["operational"]
+format = "native"
+
+[connector.state]
+model = "stateless"
+state_schema_version = "1"
+migration_hint = "No durable connector state; auth and HTTP runtime are recreated from the current config."
 
 [zones]
 home = "z:work"
 allowed_sources = ["z:work"]
 allowed_targets = ["z:work"]
-tailscale_tag = "tag:fcp-work"
+forbidden = ["z:public"]
 
 [capabilities]
-required = ["network.dns", "network.outbound"]
+required = ["network.dns", "network.egress", "network.tls.sni"]
 optional = []
-forbidden = ["system.exec", "system.privileged"]
+forbidden = ["system.exec", "network.listen", "system.privileged"]
+
+[sandbox]
+profile = "strict"
+memory_mb = 64
+cpu_percent = 25
+wall_clock_timeout_ms = 30000
+deny_exec = true
+deny_ptrace = true
+
+# The first production slice only treats hosted CircleCI SaaS on circleci.com as
+# manifest-aligned. Localhost base_url overrides remain useful for deterministic
+# tests, but CircleCI Server host overrides are not formalized in this manifest yet.
 
-[provides.operations.pipelines_list]
-summary = "List pipelines for a project"
+[provides.operations."circleci.projects.list"]
+description = "List projects visible to the authenticated CircleCI user."
+capability = "circleci.projects.read"
+risk_level = "low"
+safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
+idempotency = "none"
+input_schema = { type = "object", properties = { page_token = { type = "string", description = "Pagination token" } } }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { items = { type = "array" }, next_page_token = { type = ["string", "null"] } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.projects.list".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when you need project discovery for the authenticated CircleCI user."
+common_mistakes = ["Assuming the response is scoped to a single organization."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.pipelines.list", "circleci.health"]
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.pipelines.list"]
+description = "List recent pipelines for a specific CircleCI project."
 capability = "circleci.pipelines.read"
-risk = "low"
+risk_level = "low"
 safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
 idempotency = "none"
-
-[provides.operations.pipelines_list.network_constraints]
-host_allow = ["circleci.com"]
-port_allow = [443]
-require_tls = true
-require_sni = true
-deny_localhost = true
-deny_private_ranges = true
-deny_tailnet_ranges = true
-deny_ip_literals = true
-max_redirects = 0
-
-[provides.operations.pipelines_list.ai_hints]
-when_to_use = "When you need to list recent pipelines for a project"
-
-[provides.operations.pipelines_get]
-summary = "Get a pipeline by ID"
+input_schema = { type = "object", required = ["project_slug"], properties = { project_slug = { type = "string", description = "Project slug such as gh/org/repo or circleci/<org-id>/<project-id>" }, page_token = { type = "string", description = "Pagination token" } } }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { items = { type = "array" }, next_page_token = { type = ["string", "null"] } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.pipelines.list".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when you need the recent pipeline history for a known project."
+common_mistakes = ["Passing an unqualified repo name instead of the full project_slug."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.pipelines.get", "circleci.workflows.list"]
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.pipelines.get"]
+description = "Fetch a single pipeline by pipeline UUID."
 capability = "circleci.pipelines.read"
-risk = "low"
+risk_level = "low"
 safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
 idempotency = "none"
-
-[provides.operations.pipelines_get.network_constraints]
-host_allow = ["circleci.com"]
-port_allow = [443]
-require_tls = true
-require_sni = true
-deny_localhost = true
-deny_private_ranges = true
-deny_tailnet_ranges = true
-deny_ip_literals = true
-max_redirects = 0
-
-[provides.operations.pipelines_get.ai_hints]
-when_to_use = "When you need details about a specific pipeline"
-
-[provides.operations.pipelines_trigger]
-summary = "Trigger a new pipeline"
+input_schema = { type = "object", required = ["pipeline_id"], properties = { pipeline_id = { type = "string", description = "Pipeline UUID" } } }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { id = { type = "string" }, state = { type = "string" }, number = { type = "integer" } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.pipelines.get".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when you already have a pipeline UUID and need its current state."
+common_mistakes = ["Confusing a pipeline number with the pipeline UUID expected by the API."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.pipelines.list"]
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.pipelines.trigger"]
+description = "Trigger a new pipeline run for a known CircleCI project."
 capability = "circleci.pipelines.write"
-risk = "high"
+risk_level = "high"
 safety_tier = "risky"
-idempotency = "strict"
-
-[provides.operations.pipelines_trigger.network_constraints]
-host_allow = ["circleci.com"]
-port_allow = [443]
-require_tls = true
-require_sni = true
-deny_localhost = true
-deny_private_ranges = true
-deny_tailnet_ranges = true
-deny_ip_literals = true
-max_redirects = 0
-
-[provides.operations.pipelines_trigger.ai_hints]
-when_to_use = "When you need to trigger a new CI/CD pipeline run"
-
-[provides.operations.workflows_list]
-summary = "List workflows for a pipeline"
+requires_approval = "policy"
+idempotency = "best_effort"
+input_schema = { type = "object", required = ["project_slug"], properties = { project_slug = { type = "string", description = "Project slug such as gh/org/repo or circleci/<org-id>/<project-id>" }, branch = { type = "string", description = "Branch to run" }, tag = { type = "string", description = "Tag to run" }, parameters = { type = "object", description = "Pipeline parameters accepted by the target project config" } } }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { id = { type = "string" }, state = { type = "string" }, number = { type = "integer" } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.pipelines.trigger".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when a policy-approved automation needs to create a new pipeline run."
+common_mistakes = ["Supplying both branch and tag in the same trigger request.", "Assuming retries are safe; duplicate pipeline executions are possible."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.pipelines.list", "circleci.workflows.rerun"]
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.workflows.list"]
+description = "List workflows associated with a single pipeline."
 capability = "circleci.workflows.read"
-risk = "low"
+risk_level = "low"
 safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
 idempotency = "none"
-
-[provides.operations.workflows_list.network_constraints]
-host_allow = ["circleci.com"]
-port_allow = [443]
-require_tls = true
-require_sni = true
-deny_localhost = true
-deny_private_ranges = true
-deny_tailnet_ranges = true
-deny_ip_literals = true
-max_redirects = 0
-
-[provides.operations.workflows_list.ai_hints]
-when_to_use = "When you need to see workflows triggered by a pipeline"
-
-[provides.operations.workflows_cancel]
-summary = "Cancel a running workflow"
+input_schema = { type = "object", required = ["pipeline_id"], properties = { pipeline_id = { type = "string", description = "Pipeline UUID" }, page_token = { type = "string", description = "Pagination token" } } }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { items = { type = "array" }, next_page_token = { type = ["string", "null"] } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.workflows.list".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when you need workflow state for a specific pipeline."
+common_mistakes = ["Passing a workflow UUID where the API expects the parent pipeline UUID."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.workflows.get", "circleci.jobs.list"]
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.workflows.get"]
+description = "Fetch a single workflow by workflow UUID."
+capability = "circleci.workflows.read"
+risk_level = "low"
+safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
+idempotency = "none"
+input_schema = { type = "object", required = ["workflow_id"], properties = { workflow_id = { type = "string", description = "Workflow UUID" } } }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { id = { type = "string" }, name = { type = "string" }, status = { type = "string" } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.workflows.get".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when you already have a workflow UUID and need detailed status."
+common_mistakes = ["Treating a workflow name as a stable identifier; the API requires a UUID."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.workflows.list"]
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.workflows.cancel"]
+description = "Cancel a running CircleCI workflow."
 capability = "circleci.workflows.write"
-risk = "medium"
+risk_level = "medium"
 safety_tier = "risky"
+requires_approval = "none"
 idempotency = "strict"
-
-[provides.operations.workflows_cancel.network_constraints]
-host_allow = ["circleci.com"]
-port_allow = [443]
-require_tls = true
-require_sni = true
-deny_localhost = true
-deny_private_ranges = true
-deny_tailnet_ranges = true
-deny_ip_literals = true
-max_redirects = 0
-
-[provides.operations.workflows_cancel.ai_hints]
-when_to_use = "When you need to cancel a running workflow"
-
-[sandbox]
-profile = "strict"
-memory_mb = 64
-cpu_percent = 25
-wall_clock_timeout_ms = 30000
-deny_exec = true
-deny_ptrace = true
+input_schema = { type = "object", required = ["workflow_id"], properties = { workflow_id = { type = "string", description = "Workflow UUID" } } }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { message = { type = "string" } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.workflows.cancel".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when a running workflow must be stopped to prevent further compute or deployment work."
+common_mistakes = ["Trying to cancel a workflow that has already completed."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.workflows.get", "circleci.workflows.rerun"]
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.workflows.rerun"]
+description = "Rerun a workflow, optionally only from failed jobs."
+capability = "circleci.workflows.write"
+risk_level = "high"
+safety_tier = "risky"
+requires_approval = "policy"
+idempotency = "best_effort"
+input_schema = { type = "object", required = ["workflow_id"], properties = { workflow_id = { type = "string", description = "Workflow UUID" }, from_failed = { type = "boolean", description = "Only rerun failed jobs", default = false } } }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { message = { type = "string" } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.workflows.rerun".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when a policy-approved automation needs to create a replacement workflow execution."
+common_mistakes = ["Assuming rerun requests are deduplicated; repeated requests can create more executions."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.workflows.get", "circleci.workflows.cancel"]
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.jobs.list"]
+description = "List jobs that belong to a workflow."
+capability = "circleci.jobs.read"
+risk_level = "low"
+safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
+idempotency = "none"
+input_schema = { type = "object", required = ["workflow_id"], properties = { workflow_id = { type = "string", description = "Workflow UUID" }, page_token = { type = "string", description = "Pagination token" } } }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { items = { type = "array" }, next_page_token = { type = ["string", "null"] } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.jobs.list".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when you need job-level state inside a known workflow."
+common_mistakes = ["Passing a pipeline UUID where the API expects a workflow UUID."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.jobs.get", "circleci.workflows.list"]
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.jobs.get"]
+description = "Fetch a single job by project slug and CircleCI job number."
+capability = "circleci.jobs.read"
+risk_level = "low"
+safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
+idempotency = "none"
+input_schema = { type = "object", required = ["project_slug", "job_number"], properties = { project_slug = { type = "string", description = "Project slug such as gh/org/repo or circleci/<org-id>/<project-id>" }, job_number = { type = "integer", description = "CircleCI job number" } } }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { id = { type = "string" }, name = { type = "string" }, status = { type = "string" } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.jobs.get".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when you need details for a single known job number in a project."
+common_mistakes = ["Using a workflow UUID where the API expects a project_slug plus job number."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.jobs.list"]
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.health"]
+description = "Check CircleCI API reachability and token validity against the /me endpoint."
+capability = "circleci.projects.read"
+risk_level = "low"
+safety_tier = "safe"
+requires_approval = "none"
+idempotency = "strict"
+input_schema = { type = "object" }
+output_schema = { type = "object", properties = { status = { type = "string" } } }
+network_constraints = { host_allow = ["circleci.com"], port_allow = [443], require_sni = true, deny_localhost = true, deny_private_ranges = true, deny_tailnet_ranges = true, deny_ip_literals = true, max_redirects = 0 }
+
+[provides.operations."circleci.health".ai_hints]
+when_to_use = "Use when you need a lightweight readiness probe for hosted CircleCI connectivity."
+common_mistakes = ["Treating a successful health probe as proof that every project_slug or mutation path is permitted."]
+examples = []
+related = ["circleci.projects.list"]
